= "upcgh_miucowem_nyvvesex"

Hmm that doesn't look right. Let me reconsider — maybe the string is already rot13 of the plaintext, so decoding gives plaintext. "upcgh_miucowem_nyvvesex" isn't readable.

Wait, let me recheck letters. 'hcptu':
- h(7) -> u(20) ✓
- c(2) -> p(15) ✓
- p(15) -> c(2) ✓
- t(19) -> g(6) ✓
- u(20) -> h(7) ✓

So "hcptu" -> "upchh"? No: h->u, c->p, p->c, t->g, u->h = "upcgh". Hmm.

Maybe I should check if input was meant to look like "upcgh"... that's not a word. Let me try other direction (same since rot13 is involution).

Perhaps the intended plaintext: "much_pwned_..."? Let me try: "hcptu" reversed = "utpch"? No.

Actually wait — maybe I misread. Let me recount: h-c-p-t-u. Yes.

Hmm. Let me check 'zvhpbjrz': z->m, v->i, h->u, p->c, b->o, j->w, r->e, z->m = "miucowem". Not a word.

And 'aliirfrk': a->n, l->y, i->v, i->v, r->e, f->s, r->e, k->x = "nyvvesex". Not words.

Something's off. Maybe the rot13 strings encode something else, or I need to re-examine. Let me try decoding the whole string with codecs rot13 carefully.

Maybe the actual decoded answer is supposed to be readable. Let me see if some clever interpretation works. Or maybe the encoded form gives "much_pwned_letters..." Let me try the reverse — what would rot13-encode to give "much_pwned_letters"?

"much" -> zhpu. Hmm input starts "hcpt". Not matching.

Maybe the intended plaintext rot13 input is rot13 of "upcgh_miucowem_nyvvesex"? Let me look at "upcgh" — could be a typo. Or maybe try: what if the string was meant "uchgt" or simil
